articles.title: NEET paper leak claim: Caller shows ‘question paper’ on video call, demands Rs 30k
articles.description: Ajmer police are investigating an alleged attempt to sell the NEET question paper. A student reported receiving a video call from an unidentified person displaying the paper and demanding Rs 30,000. A local ABVP member also received a similar offer. Authorities are working to identify the caller and confirm the paper's authenticity, suspecting a fraudulent scheme ahead of the NEET re-exam.
articles.full_text: Ajmer police are investigating an alleged attempt to sell the NEET question paper. A student reported receiving a video call from an unidentified person displaying the paper and demanding Rs 30,000. A local ABVP member also received a similar offer. Authorities are working to identify the caller and confirm the paper's authenticity, suspecting a fraudulent scheme ahead of the NEET re-exam.

articles.title: CJP overnight protest: Dipke claims authorities cut off water, electricity at Jantar Mantar
articles.description: Protesters at Jantar Mantar, demanding Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation over examination controversies, face water supply issues in restrooms. The Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) highlighted the lack of basic facilities for demonstrators, contrasting it with the minister's situation. Meanwhile, the NEET-UG 2026 re-examination is underway nationwide, with CJP inviting aspirants to join their protest post-exam.

articles.title: UK detains Indian captain of sanctioned ship, family in Uttarakhand pleads for release
articles.description: An Indian captain, Ajay Pant, has been arrested in the UK and charged with breaching sanctions after his Russian oil tanker was intercepted in the English Channel. The vessel, en route to India, was found to be stateless. Pant's family in Uttarakhand has appealed to the Indian government for his safe return, while authorities are coordinating efforts for his release.
articles.full_text: An Indian captain, Ajay Pant, has been arrested in the UK and charged with breaching sanctions after his Russian oil tanker was intercepted in the English Channel. The vessel, en route to India, was found to be stateless. Pant's family in Uttarakhand has appealed to the Indian government for his safe return, while authorities are coordinating efforts for his release.

articles.title: Anthropic's 3k employees have one question after US 'banned' its most powerful AI models
articles.description: Anthropic's 3,000 employees are still confused nearly a week after the White House gave the company under 90 minutes to pull its most powerful AI models, Fable 5 and Mythos 5, over vague national security concerns. As The New York Times reported from internal chats, staff suspect they're being targeted rather than reviewed. Triggered by an Amazon paper, the shutdown has left engineers asking whether the Trump administration simply doesn't want Anthropic to exist.

articles.title: Petrol, diesel rates may ease as cheaper crude arrives, says oil minister Puri
articles.description: Petrol and diesel prices may soon see a dip as India receives cheaper crude oil, Union Minister Hardeep Singh Puri indicated. He defended domestic fuel pricing, stating increases have been minimal globally despite market volatility. The government has absorbed significant costs through excise duty cuts, shielding consumers from the full impact of rising international rates. Puri also highlighted Sonbhadra's development progress.

articles.title: 3 Indian-flagged oil tankers cross Strait of Hormuz, head home with 94 crew members
articles.description: Three Indian oil tankers, carrying over 8.6 lakh metric tonnes of cargo and 94 crew members, have successfully navigated the Strait of Hormuz and are heading home. This safe passage follows a US decision to lift its blockade on Iran, allowing resumed movement through the vital waterway. The government assures continued efforts to protect India's maritime and energy interests, with the vessels expected to reach Indian ports next week.

articles.title: Monsoon, El Niño and market trends: NSE highlights key risks for India’s 2026 economy
articles.description: India's 2026 economic outlook hinges on monsoon performance, with El Niño posing a significant risk of deficient rainfall impacting agriculture. Meanwhile, the nation's equity investor base is rapidly expanding, becoming younger and more diverse, with a notable surge in participation from smaller cities. Despite this growth, trading activity remains heavily concentrated among a small group of large investors across all market segments.

articles.title: Milk prices may rise again by July as El Niño threatens fodder supply
articles.description: Dairy consumers might face another price hike by July or August, with industry experts warning of a potential 3-4% increase. This follows a recent 2-3% rise in May. Concerns are mounting over El Niño potentially worsening fodder shortages and impacting milk production, prompting the Maharashtra government to urge farmers to boost fodder cultivation. Major dairies are closely monitoring the situation.

articles.title: ODI tickets worth Rs 52.36 lakh went unsold: Khanna
articles.description: "Tickets worth Rupees 52,36,000 were unsold. Why all the tickets were not sold-out? DDCA started online ticket sale quite late that too without proper advertisement," Khanna claimed in a media release.

articles.title: IIT resume advisory sparks debate over removing JEE and GATE ranks from CVs
articles.description: A reportedly circulated advisory from an IIT Career Development Centre, said to be based on All IIT Placement Committee guidelines, has sparked debate after asking students to remove JEE and GATE ranks, scores and percentiles from resumes. Supporters say the move promotes skill-based hiring and resume uniformity, while critics argue it suppresses hard-earned achievements. The issue gained traction on social media, where users remain divided over whether placements should prioritize entrance exam performance or college accomplishments.

articles.title: AFCAT 02/2026 application window closes today for Indian Air Force vacancies; apply here
articles.description: The Indian Air Force has closed the application window for AFCAT 02/2026 today, June 21, 2026, for 379 vacancies across the Flying Branch and Ground Duty Technical and Non-Technical branches. The recruitment includes AFCAT Entry, NCC Special Entry and GATE Score Entry for courses beginning in July 2027. Eligible candidates can apply for Permanent Commission and Short Service Commission entries. The selection process includes a written test, AFSB assessment, document verification and medical examination.
articles.full_text: The Indian Air Force has closed the application window for AFCAT 02/2026 today, June 21, 2026, for 379 vacancies across the Flying Branch and Ground Duty Technical and Non-Technical branches. The recruitment includes AFCAT Entry, NCC Special Entry and GATE Score Entry for courses beginning in July 2027. Eligible candidates can apply for Permanent Commission and Short Service Commission entries. The selection process includes a written test, AFSB assessment, document verification and medical examination.

articles.title: NEET UG 2026 re-exam time: NTA issues final advisory on entry rules, documents, and centre guidelines
articles.description: NEET UG 2026 is being conducted today across 5,440 centres in 551 Indian cities and 14 overseas centres. NTA has reminded candidates that entry begins at 11:00 AM and closes at 1:30 PM, with no admission permitted afterwards. Aspirants must carry their admit card, a valid photo ID and two passport-size photographs. The agency has also advised candidates to avoid prohibited items, stay hydrated, reach centres early and rely only on official information.

articles.title: UP Police Constable answer key 2026 released at uppbpb.gov.in; objections accepted until June 23
articles.description: The Uttar Pradesh Police Recruitment and Promotion Board (UPPRPB) has released the provisional answer key for the UP Police Constable Recruitment Examination 2026 conducted for 32,679 posts. Candidates who appeared in the offline examination held on June 08, 09 and 10 can now check their responses and estimate their scores. The objection window is open from June 20 to June 23. The final answer key, after scrutiny of objections, will be used to prepare the recruitment result.
articles.full_text: The Uttar Pradesh Police Recruitment and Promotion Board (UPPRPB) has released the provisional answer key for the UP Police Constable Recruitment Examination 2026 conducted for 32,679 posts. The final answer key, after scrutiny of objections, will be used to prepare the recruitment result.

articles.title: Jennifer Aniston and Courteney Cox honour James Burrows
articles.description: With a heavy heart, we announce the passing of the legendary director James Burrows at 85, celebrated for his masterpieces 'Friends' and 'Cheers'. Jennifer Aniston and Courteney Cox were among many who honored him with emotional tributes, highlighting his role as a father figure. Beyond directing, Burrows provided invaluable life lessons, leaving an indelible mark not just on television comedy, but on the hearts and minds of all he influenced.

articles.title: Manoj on film industry monopoly: 'Big films take most shows'
articles.description: Manoj Bajpayee passionately champions a just film exhibition framework within Indian cinema, asserting that large-budget movies dominate cinema screens through coercive tactics directed at distributors and exhibitors. He emphasizes the essential role of governmental support to ensure smaller independent films get the necessary visibility, pointing to the triumph of Marathi cinema bolstered by state-supported screen allocations.

articles.title: Gordon Ramsay's daughter announces her pregnancy
articles.description: Celebrity chef Gordon Ramsay is set to become a grandfather as his daughter, Holly, announced her pregnancy with husband Adam Peaty. The couple revealed they are expecting a baby girl in December 2026. The joyous news was shared on Instagram, drawing warm congratulations from family and friends, including a delighted Gordon Ramsay who expressed his excitement about becoming an 'overexcited Grandad'.

articles.title: 'Toxic: A Fairytale for Grown-Ups' hints at a big update
articles.description: Fans are buzzing with anticipation as the 'Toxic' team dropped a cryptic "Got plans for tomorrow?" hinting at a major update on June 21. Speculation is rife about release dates or promotional material for Yash's action thriller, directed by Geetu Mohandas. The film, featuring a star-studded cast, has seen its release timeline shift multiple times, with the latest delay reportedly for better global distribution.
